summary: First-pass full backend + frontend testing of LifeLedger. 16/16 pytest cases pass covering auth (owner login/wrong-password/me), public GETs (settings=2003-08-28, finance/summary shape, list arrays), auth protection on all mutating endpoints, Savings/Transactions/Business CRUD with correct finance summary recomputation, and Daily-log 9AM rule (set_before_9am correctly evaluates against Europe/Amsterdam time; status='complete' only when tasks done AND set_before_9am). Frontend UI verified end-to-end: sidebar nav (dashboard/daily/life/finances/business), Life-in-Weeks grid renders, Daily calendar day cells render and open editor, read-only mode correctly hides add-savings/add-txn/add-business/save-day, Owner login via /login page shows all owner controls and Sign out.

issues: Calendar initially shows the month reported by the container clock (currently July 2026 in the preview env). Not a bug per se, but worth noting: verify container time zone / system date reflect production expectations.

critical_code_review_comments: server.py: /api/auth/logout does not clear any cookie server-side. It's cosmetic since the frontend uses localStorage JWT, but if cookie-based auth is later re-enabled, this endpoint will silently fail to invalidate.
server.py: JWT has no jti/blacklist. A logout endpoint that returns {ok:true} without token revocation is acceptable for a single-user app but should be documented.
server.py: TransactionInput has no validator on `type` (income|expense). A bad value would silently be stored and ignored by summary aggregation. Consider Literal['income','expense'].
server.py: compute_status may return status='logged' when tasks_total==0 but did_right/did_wrong are set - fine, but reviewing UX intent worth confirming with product.
Frontend: uses localStorage JWT + Authorization header (per spec). Backend also accepts cookie 'access_token' but login response doesn't set it — keep in mind if a future cross-tab feature is added.
server.py uses deprecated @app.on_event startup/shutdown. Consider migrating to lifespan context (non-blocking now).
test_credentials: Owner credentials loaded from env ADMIN_EMAIL/ADMIN_PASSWORD.
seed_data_creation: Tests use TEST_-prefixed data and clean up after themselves (savings, transactions, business, daily log tasks for today).
context_for_next_testing_agent: backend_test.py in /app/backend/tests uses pytest-xdist (2 workers) via existing pytest.ini. All 16 tests currently pass. Preview URL from frontend/.env is used as BASE_URL. 9AM branch is auto-detected using Europe/Amsterdam so the test is portable across time-of-day.
